title: [Feature]: Add safe plugin discovery for external sinks
problem: The package can grow additional built-in sinks, but long-term extensibility may require third-party sink plugins. Dynamic discovery can introduce supply-chain and code-execution risks if arbitrary configuration can select modules or classes without a strict policy.
proposal: Design safe plugin discovery using explicit entry points, allow-listed plugin names, compatibility metadata, and a certification requirement. Discovery should be optional, disabled or tightly controlled by default, and documented as an advanced extension path rather than the primary way to use production sinks.
users: External maintainers building additional sinks, operators evaluating third-party connectors, and core maintainers protecting the public API.
delivery_semantics: Plugins must implement the same Sink protocol and must never ACK messages. Certification must prove commit-then-ACK remains core-owned and that plugin sinks return success only after durable destination success.
security: Plugin loading is a code-execution boundary. Configuration must not allow arbitrary imports. Documentation must explain dependency trust, review, version compatibility, and why untrusted plugins are unsafe.
acceptance: A plugin discovery design is documented before implementation.
Discovery uses explicit allow lists or trusted entry-point metadata.
Plugin sinks must pass certification tests before production recommendation.
Dynamic imports from untrusted config remain prohibited.
Public API compatibility tests cover the extension surface.
tests: Add unit tests for registry behavior, rejected unknown plugins, compatibility metadata, and certification hooks if code is added. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/sink-framework.md, docs/development.md, docs/security.md, docs/public-api.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after a released design or implementation. Include release tag, plugin policy, tests run, and follow-up issues for plugin certification or marketplace guidance.
